# ggaction

ggaction is a JavaScript chart-authoring library built around immutable,
hierarchical actions. This file points to canonical documentation for humans
and language models.

## Start

- Documentation index: ./index.md
- Getting started: ./getting-started.md
- Complete scatterplot tutorial: ./tutorials/scatterplot.md
- Complete line chart tutorial: ./tutorials/line-chart.md
- Complete histogram tutorial: ./tutorials/histogram.md
- Complete bar chart tutorial (grouped example): ./tutorials/grouped-bar.md
- Chart recipe index: ./recipes/index.md
- Scatterplot recipe: ./recipes/scatterplot.md
- Line chart recipe: ./recipes/line-chart.md
- Histogram recipe: ./recipes/histogram.md
- Bar chart recipe (grouped example): ./recipes/bar-chart.md
- Troubleshooting inference, layout, and scale errors: ./troubleshooting.md
- Complete action reference: ./reference/actions.md
- Supported features: ./supported-features.md

## Core concepts

- ChartProgram and immutability: ./concepts/chart-program.md
- Semantic and graphical state: ./concepts/semantic-and-graphics.md
- Actions and trace trees: ./concepts/actions-and-trace.md

## API

- Canvas: ./api/canvas.md
- Data: ./api/data.md
- Marks: ./api/marks.md
- Encodings: ./api/encodings.md
- Position encodings: ./api/position-encodings.md

- Temporal line positions: ./api/position/temporal.md
- Ordinal bar positions: ./api/position/ordinal-bars.md
- Histogram positions: ./api/position/histogram.md
- Advanced ordinal offsets: ./api/position/offsets.md
- Series encodings: ./api/series-encodings.md
- Constant appearance: ./api/appearance.md
- Scale options: ./api/scales.md
- Coordinates: ./api/coordinates.md
- Guides: ./api/guides.md
- Axes: ./api/axes.md
- Grids: ./api/grids.md
- Legends: ./api/legends.md
- Titles: ./api/titles.md
- Rendering: ./api/rendering.md
- Advanced axis components: ./advanced/axis-components.md
- Action authoring: ./extension/action-authoring.md
- Primitive extension API: ./extension/primitives.md

## Architectural invariants

- Every action accepts one plain option object and returns a new ChartProgram.
- Existing programs and caller-owned inputs are never mutated.
- semanticSpec records chart meaning.
- graphicSpec stores fully materialized backend-neutral graphics.
- There is no automatic semantic-to-graphic compiler.
- Domain actions explicitly invoke every required graphical materialization.
- Renderers read graphicSpec only; they do not infer from semanticSpec.
- Primitive graphics support heterogeneous drawable collections whose children
  each carry a concrete circle, rect, line, text, or path type.
- Backend-neutral paths may be open/stroked or `closed: true` and filled; a
  closed path may combine fill and stroke.
- Nested wrapped actions form the hierarchical trace.
- Positional encodings create and attach coordinates; guide actions only read them.
- encodeRadius is fixed circle appearance, not a Polar radial encoding.
- encodeSize stores a quantitative semantic encoding and maps its automatic
  domain to point areas `[24, 196]`; circles and squares use equal areas.
- encodeShape stores a nominal semantic encoding and materializes circle/square
  typed children. encodeOpacity is a fixed graphical constant.
- One rematerializePointMark action combines x, y, color, size, shape, and
  opacity independently of authoring call order and after Canvas changes.
- Temporal line encodeX resolves a time scale but does not yet materialize path points.
- Aggregate line encodeY computes mean groups and explicitly materializes sorted paths.
- Line encodeColor and encodeStrokeDash regroup and style concrete series paths.
- createBarMark stores a semantic bar and creates an empty rect collection.
- filterData creates a named immutable derived dataset, defaults its source to
  current data, stores filter provenance, and materializes source-order rows.
- createRegressionData creates immutable grouped linear OLS predictions at
  observed unique x values and Student-t mean-response confidence bounds; it
  defaults source to current data and confidence to 0.95.
- Binned bar encodeX stores maxBins, resolves a nice linear x domain/range, and deliberately leaves rect materialization for later y encoding.
- Ordinal bar encodeX requires fieldType "ordinal", preserves first-appearance domain order, resolves equal band geometry, and deliberately leaves rect materialization for later aggregate/group actions.
- Ordinal bar encodeY requires a quantitative field, defaults to mean with stack null, resolves y from x-category means, and keeps rects empty until grouping semantics exist.
- encodeXOffset is an advanced action that resolves nominal slots inside one ordinal x bandwidth; grouped color layout is expected to call it for ordinary chart authors.
- Ordinal mean bars require encodeColor layout "group"; it stores stack null, calls encodeXOffset as a wrapped child, and recomputes y from x/color means while rects remain empty until width is authored.
- encodeBarWidth defaults band to 0.72, stores immutable graphical materialization config outside semanticSpec, and materializes concrete grouped rects in x-domain then color-domain order; Canvas changes explicitly rebuild them.
- Ordinal x axes default to every domain value at band centers, reject count-based sampling, support explicit domain subsets, and rematerialize after Canvas changes.
- Every categorical legend defaults to the right regardless of mark or chart type; pass position: "bottom" explicitly for a horizontal legend.
- Bar encodeY infers the binned x field, stores count/zero-stack, resolves both scales, and materializes concrete rects for non-empty bins.
- encodeHistogram is a thin aggregate whose direct children are binned bar encodeX and count/zero-stack encodeY.
- Bar encodeColor resolves an ordinal scale and rematerializes each bin as zero-stacked category rects in color-domain order; the y domain remains based on total bin counts.
- createAxes supports linear and UTC time scales, histogram bin-boundary x ticks, and aggregate-aware title inference.
- createGrid defaults to horizontal lines, supports optional vertical lines, reuses compatible axis ticks, and inserts concrete lines before related marks.
- createLegend materializes a categorical right-side legend by default; explicit bottom placement is available, and layered symbol recipes support line, point, swatch, and line-plus-point compositions.
- createTitle stores semantic title text and materializes a plot-aligned top title with an optional subtitle.
- createGuides calls applicable createAxes, createGrid, and createLegend wrapped actions in that order; it never creates chart titles.
- For grouped bars, createGuides infers an ordinal x axis, quantitative y axis, horizontal grid, and right-side color legend while forwarding explicit child options and false opt-outs.

## Current scope

The documented build is version 0.0.0-dev and is not published to npm. Use the
repository source directly.

The implemented end-to-end Chart API covers Cartesian point scatterplots,
temporal mean-aggregate line charts, stacked histograms, and ordinal bar charts
with a grouped layout. Every public example supports browser Canvas and PNG
output.

- Scatterplots use quantitative x/y, nominal color/shape, quantitative size,
  and fixed radius or opacity.
- Line charts use temporal x, mean-aggregate y, color/strokeDash series, guides,
  and an optional chart title.
- Histograms use atomic `encodeHistogram`, optional color stacking, inferred
  guides, and explicit bottom legend placement in the public example.
- Bar charts use ordinal x, mean-aggregate y, grouped color/xOffset layout,
  band-based width, inferred guides, and a right-side legend.

`createGuides` composes compatible axes, grid, and categorical legend actions.
`createTitle` remains separate. Cartesian grids are also available directly
through `createGrid`.
Do not assume roadmap features are implemented.
